strategy: This is a 58,300-health gauntlet boss, so run it as a team: a hitter, supports who blade and Feint, and a healer. Doom and Gloom goes up at the start, so plan to burst rather than heal through it.
Do not cast Dark Pact. An observed cheat has stripped all positive charms from the team, both times right after a Dark Pact.
Expect the 99-round Darkness aura (-50% accuracy) by the end of round 1, so bring accuracy enchants or Sniper. While you carry Darkness, casting a spell hands you a Torch card.
Protect your traps. Feinting Nyx (including Mass Feint and pet feints) makes her Shadow-Cleanse all traps off herself, so Indemnity a key trap or blade the hitter instead.
Stack blades and Indemnity-protected Feints, keep accuracy up, then land one big hit; she also opens with immediate Dark Nova, Dark Fiend, or Dark Shepherd hits, so keep the team healed.
cheatNotes: An observed cheat stripped all positive charms from the team, both times right after someone cast Dark Pact. Avoid Dark Pact here.
Doom and Gloom goes up at the start of combat, halving your healing. Burst rather than heal through it.
By the end of round 1 (or on a late join) she lands a 99-round Darkness aura: -50% accuracy and likely reduced power-pip chance. Bring accuracy enchants.
She opens with immediate Dark Nova, Dark Fiend, or Dark Shepherd hits, or a Natural Attack that also steals your pips.
Casting a spell on yourself only triggers a harmless interrupt line nudging you to target allies. No real effect.
Feinting Nyx makes her Shadow-Cleanse all traps off herself. Protect a key trap with Indemnity or skip traps.
Indemnity on your trap prevents that cleanse.
Mass Feint (+75%) still triggers the trap-cleanse.
Pet-cast feints trigger the trap-cleanse too.
While you carry the Darkness aura, successfully casting a spell hands you a Torch card.
